scope_note: QA Phase-Conjugate Holographic Associative Memory Cert [519]. Primary sources: (Soffer, 1986) DOI 10.1364/OL.11.000118; (Owechko, 1987) IEEE JQE 25(3):619-634; (Hopfield, 1982) DOI 10.1073/pnas.79.8.2554; (Levin, 2021) target morphology as attractor. Soffer, Dunning, Owechko, Marom (1986) 'Associative holographic memory with feedback using phase-conjugate mirrors' Opt. Lett. 11(2):118-120; Owechko (1987) 'Nonlinear holographic associative memories' IEEE J. Quantum Electron.; Hopfield (1982) PNAS 79(8):2554-2558; Levin (2021) Front. Syst. Neurosci. CLAIM (falsifiable, empirically demonstrated): the exact QA phase conjugator of cert [518] composes into a content-addressable associative memory whose stored patterns are attractors. Patterns/probes are QA phase vectors in {1,...,m}^N; the phase-conjugate overlap of probe x with stored pattern k is C_k=#{i: qa_add(x_i,qa_neg(P_k_i))==identity(=m)} (== phase-match count, grounded in [518]); recall is holographic playback (per-site superposition of stored patterns weighted by sharpened overlap, argmax phase), iterated to a fixed point. DEMONSTRATED (reference impl qa_phase_conjugate_memory.py, m=24, N=120, axiom-linter clean): (D1) stored patterns are EXACT fixed points; (D2) exact recall 1.000 up to 80% probe corruption, 0.995 at 90%, 0.855 at 95% (breaks near the alphabet noise floor); (D3) capacity: exact recall 1.000 at load K/N up to 4.27 (512 patterns) for random patterns -- crosstalk ~N/m per pair, so the mod-24 alphabet makes patterns near-orthogonal and capacity is very high but NOT unbounded (degrades as patterns approach identical); (D4) correlated patterns (noisy variants of 4 shared prototypes, 70% shared) still recall 1.000 to K=128; (D5) content-addressable: probe at 35% corruption converges to the EXACT nearest stored pattern 1.000; (D6) DISTORTION TOLERANCE via the [518] property: a global phase screen phi on the probe breaks naive recall (0.000) but phase-locked recall -- the resonator locks onto the compensation phase that maximizes overlap, the discrete analog of a phase-conjugate mirror self-adjusting to the distorting medium -- recovers the pattern 1.000 for all phi (the distortion-correction theorem giving distortion-tolerant recall). Discrete analog of the optical phase-conjugate resonator (Soffer 1986; Owechko 1987) and of Levin's morphogenetic attractor recall. Checks PC_OVERLAP/FIXED_POINTS/RECALL/CONTENT_ADDRESSABLE/DISTORTION_TOLERANT/NAIVE_DISTORTION_FAILS/A1_RANGE/SRC/F. HONEST LIMIT: recall is exact-match/overlap based; capacity is high because random large-alphabet patterns are near-orthogonal, and it degrades toward chance as stored patterns are made nearly identical or corruption reaches the noise floor -- not a claim of unbounded capacity. Builds on cert [518] (the exact FWM conjugator). Author: Will Dale + Claude 2026-07-08. All phase state integer in {1,...,m} (A1/S2); additive identity is m never 0; correlation/vote weights are float observer-layer scores never fed back as QA state (Theorem NT).
